points.label: Help mode: usage question answered from the documentation
points.summary: Introspection is now also the interactive help: with developer mode on, a plain usage question gets the relevant documentation passages injected (a new 'documentation' line in the introspect step details) and the answer should mirror the docs near-verbatim — headings/structure from the documentation, with symbol references (file path + GitHub link) attached to code names it shows. PASS: the composer question below yields a docs-grounded answer about workspace links (not a generic LLM guess), the introspect activity step lists at least one documentation file (e.g. docs/WORKSPACE-SECURITY.md), and any code symbol shown carries a source reference.

actions.text: Make sure the Developer mode knob is ON, then send the prefilled question.
How do I share a secure workspace link?

points.label: Help mode: documentation screenshot renders inline in the answer
points.summary: Doc images now ride into answers: the docs corpus rewrites documentation image references to served /introspect/docs-img/ URLs, and markdown rendering allows exactly those same-origin images (isSafeDocImage). Asking about the first-run data notice should retrieve docs/ENCRYPTION.md's section that embeds the rver-app.png screenshot, and the model is instructed to reproduce image lines verbatim. PASS: the answer shows the actual screenshot inline (styled, rounded, fitting the bubble) with its caption — not a broken image, not a bare URL. FAIL if the image line appears but renders as text or a broken icon.

actions.text: Developer mode ON. The answer should include the Se/rver first-run data-notice screenshot from the encryption doc, rendered as a real image.
Show me what the documentation says about the Se/rver first-run data notice, exactly as the documentation presents it, images included.

points.label: Help mode: Swedish usage question retrieves the English docs (dense index)
points.summary: The docs dense index is multilingual e5, so a Swedish help question must retrieve the right English documentation passages (the lexical fallback can't cross languages — dense is the DRS path). PASS: the Swedish question below produces a docs-grounded answer about chat-history encryption (AES-GCM, key never at rest beside ciphertext), with the introspect step's documentation line naming docs/ENCRYPTION.md or similar. FAIL if the answer is generic/unanchored or the documentation line is missing.

points.label: Help mode: follow-up escalates from docs into a source-code proof
points.summary: The two-layer contract: after a docs-answered question, a follow-up that asks for PROOF must escalate into the source (the read loop / native tools), and the conclusion must quote actual code and cite file paths (e.g. public/js/vault-core.js) — not just re-assert the documentation. PASS: send the first question, then the follow-up; the second answer grounds its conclusion in quoted source lines with file paths (source-investigation steps visible in the activity), and does NOT merely repeat the doc's claim. Run this after the first point in the same chat.

actions.text: First ask: 'How do I store a project in the vault?' — then follow up with the prefilled proof question in the SAME chat.
Prove that the server never sees my vault secret — show me the actual code.
